<h4 class=name> Dr. Matthew Adam Turner - Director of Infrastructure</h4>

![](/imagens/bio/MatthewTurner.png){.float-left height=210px}

Matthew is a quantitative social scientist accelerating translational research
to make sustainable societies the more lucrative choice. Sustainability includes
public health, since a healthy population is required for participation in
sustainable practices. Matthew began developing this translational research
infrastructure during his time as a Pandemic Preparedness Hub Fellow with
Stanford Medicine. 

Matthew received has received the Cognitive Science Society
Conference’s Diversity and Social Inequality award for demonstrating the
practical importance of minority groups for accelerating public health
dissemination. He showed that minority groups tend to be
more visible in their communities and so make ideal teachers. 

Matthew
has developed and taught new courses in quantitative social science for the
Stanford Doerr School of Sustainability, received his PhD in Cognitive and
Information Sciences in 2021 from the University of California, Merced, and
holds a M.S. in Applied Physics from Rice University with a dual B.S. in
Math and Physics from Syracuse University.
